classification.reasoning: The patch introduces additional logic to handle out-of-memory (OOM) situations by broadcasting kill requests to other nodes when certain failures occur during distributed result streaming. The changes involve adding new imports, fields, constructor parameters, and method calls to existing classes, as well as a new static method for broadcasting kill requests. The patch is mostly additive and modifies existing control flow without deep structural refactoring or redesign. The localization evidence shows most hunks were found via 'git_pickaxe' with high confidence, indicating some fuzzy matching due to added code rather than exact string replacements. Overall, the patch complexity aligns with TYPE_II, involving whitespace/formatting drift and moderate code additions requiring fuzzy matching. The token budget estimate accounts for the moderate size and complexity of the patch and the need to carefully integrate the new kill logic into existing distributed execution code.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a static import for the broadcastKill method from TransportDistributedResultAction.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable direct usage of broadcastKill method for sending kill requests without qualifying the class name.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Imported KillJobsNodeRequest and KillResponse classes related to job kill actions.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To support job kill functionality by using these classes in the DistributingConsumer.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added two new fields: killNodeAction (an ActionExecutor for kill requests) and localNodeId (String).
hunk_descriptions.intent: To hold references needed to send kill requests to nodes and identify the local node for job cancellation.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Modified constructor signature to accept killNodeAction and localNodeId parameters.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable the DistributingConsumer to send kill requests and know the local node identity during construction.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Initialized the newly added killNodeAction and localNodeId fields in the constructor.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To properly assign the kill action executor and local node ID for later use in the instance.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Replaced logic on non-failure requests to broadcast a kill signal to downstream nodes, close iterator, and complete future exceptionally.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: DistributingConsumer, method handling request completion (likely a callback or response handler)
hunk_descriptions.intent: To ensure that when an error occurs, all downstream nodes are notified to kill the job to prevent hanging or inconsistent states.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added imports for KillJobsNodeAction, KillJobsNodeRequest, and KillResponse related to job kill functionality.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable the factory to create DistributingConsumer instances with kill job capabilities.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a new field killNodeAction of type ActionExecutor for kill job requests.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To hold the executor responsible for sending kill job requests to nodes, enabling DistributingConsumer creation with kill support.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Initialized killNodeAction field with a lambda that executes KillJobsNodeAction on the node's client.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To provide a concrete implementation for sending kill job requests through the node client.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Modified the create method call to pass killNodeAction and local node ID as additional parameters to DistributingConsumer constructor.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To supply the necessary kill action executor and local node identifier to the DistributingConsumer instances it creates.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of java.util.UUID to support job ID handling.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable usage of UUID type for job identifiers in this class.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Changed the constructor call to pass 'true' for the parameter controlling whether requests are forcefully handled (previously false).
hunk_descriptions.intent: To ensure that all requests are always handled to prevent jobs from getting stuck due to unhandled requests.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Replaced explicit kill request construction and execution code with a call to the new broadcastKill static method to send kill requests when no RootTask is found for a job. Added the broadcastKill static method to encapsulate sending kill requests to all nodes except the local one.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: TransportDistributedResultAction.handleRequest method (failure handling), plus new static method broadcastKill
hunk_descriptions.intent: To centralize and reuse the logic for broadcasting kill requests to nodes when job context is missing, preventing handler local-merge from getting stuck.

hunk_descriptions.intent: To hold references needed to send kill requests and identify the local node for job cancellation.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Modified constructor signature to accept killNodeAction and localNodeId parameters.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable the consumer to send kill requests and know the local node identity during instantiation.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Assigned the new killNodeAction and localNodeId fields in the constructor body.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To initialize the new fields for use within the instance.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Replaced logic on non-failure requests to broadcast a kill signal to downstream nodes, close iterator, and complete the future exceptionally instead of continuing normally.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: DistributingConsumer, method handling request completion (likely a callback or response handler)
hunk_descriptions.intent: To ensure that when an error occurs, all downstream nodes are informed to kill the job to prevent hanging or inconsistent state.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added imports for KillJobsNodeAction, KillJobsNodeRequest, and KillResponse classes.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To support creation and execution of kill job requests within the factory.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a new field killNodeAction of type ActionExecutor for kill job requests.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To hold a reference to the kill job action executor for use when creating DistributingConsumer instances.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Initialized killNodeAction field with a lambda that executes KillJobsNodeAction on the node's client.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To provide a mechanism to send kill job requests to nodes via the client.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Updated the call to DistributingConsumer constructor to pass killNodeAction and local node ID as additional parameters.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To propagate the kill action executor and local node identity to the consumer for job cancellation capabilities.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of java.util.UUID.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To support usage of UUID type in this class.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Changed the constructor call of the superclass to set the 'canTripCircuitBreaker' flag from false to true.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable circuit breaker tripping on transport layer requests, likely for better resource protection.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Refactored failure handling when no RootTask is found for a job: replaced inline kill request logic with a call to broadcastKill method; extracted broadcastKill as a static utility method to send kill requests to all nodes except the local one.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To centralize and reuse logic for broadcasting kill requests on job failures, ensuring all nodes are notified to prevent stuck jobs.
